channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: Defence spending, Erdogan-style: Guns for NATO leaders
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, July 10: the French press react to another World Cup victory for the Blues, this time against Morocco. In other news, the Boeing jet gifted to Donald Trump by the Qatari state has occasioned new controversy, as it may not have the necessary safety features to transport a President. And, finally, NATO leaders express gratitude and confusion at President Erdogan's parting gift: an engraved pistol with 6 live bullets.
item.dc:creator: Antonia KERRIGAN

item.title: What future for Gaza after Hamas dissolves governing body?
item.description: Hamas announced this week that it had dissolved the de facto government, saying it was ready to hand over power to a group of technocrats appointed by Donald Trump's Board of Peace. Israel dismissed the move as a stunt. But with the Israeli military continuing to bomb Gaza despite the ostensible ceasefire, while controlling more than 60 per cent of the territory, and with the technocratic committee still outside the enclave, who is really in charge?
item.dc:creator: Nadia MASSIH

item.title: Iran had 'specific' plan to kill Trump, US media report citing Israeli intel
item.description: Iran planned to kill US President Donald Trump in a fresh plot unveiled by Israel, several US media outlets report. According to Israeli intelligence, the plan was “specific” and may explain why Trump chose to switch planes when leaving the NATO summit in Turkey earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Spain confront Belgium in World Cup quarter-finals, winner to face France
item.description: Spain and Belgium are set to face off in the World Cup quarter-finals on Friday. The winner of the match will go on to be tested against Kylian Mbappé's formidable France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France book World Cup semi-final place with win over Morocco thanks to Mbappé, Dembélé
item.description: Les Bleus advanced to the World Cup semi-finals for the third consecutive time on Thursday. Didier Deschamps' side struggled for the first 45 minutes against Morocco’s defence before making the breakthrough in the second half thanks to Kylian Mbappé and Ousmane Dembélé (2-0). They will face Spain or Belgium for a spot in the final.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: 🔴Bayeux Tapestry arrives safely in UK after loan from France
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Bayeux Tapestry arrives safely in UK after loan from France
item.description: The medieval Bayeux Tapestry arrived in London after almost 1,000 years early on Friday. The tapestry is on loan from France for a year and will be exhibited at the British Museum. Its journey has been surrounded by secrecy due to security concerns.
item.source: © Lou Benoist, pool, AFP/ File picture
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: N. Korea says will bolster nuclear force, expand S. Korea spy activities in ‘radical way’
item.description: North Korea plans to boost its nuclear force “both in quality and quantity”, state media reported on Friday, citing a decision from a military commission meeting. The country also announced plans to bolster its spying activities in South Korea “in a radical way”.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Moroccan fans meet to watch the quarter-final against France in Boston
item.description: Boston is home to a large Moroccan community, one of the largest in the US. And they were out in force for the quarter-final against France.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates as fresh missile strikes threaten Middle East ceasefire
item.description: The United States launched fresh air strikes on Iran early Thursday, prompting Tehran to fire missiles at its neighbours Bahrain, Qatar, Kuwait and Jordan hosting US military forces, officials said. The exchanges threatened a fragile interim ceasefire aimed at ending the Middle East conflict, with explosions also reported near Iran's nuclear power plant.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé clears air around penalty against Morocco
item.description:
Though he found the back of the net later in the match, France captain Kylian Mbappé criticised himself for losing focus and not scoring from the penalty spot in the first half against Morocco.
When asked whether the 2026 French squad was the strongest French side he had seen, Mbappé was quick not to let the team rest on their laurels of going unbeaten in the 2026 edition of the FIFA World Cup.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup: ZaÎre-Emery overcome with emotion after first France apppearance
item.description:
Coming off the bench to play for Les Bleus, French midfielder Warren Zaïre-Emery was overcome with emotion while talking to reporters after a commanding 2-0 victory over Morocco.
The 20-year-old reflected on his stellar season, both at his club PSG and for the French national team, adding that the work was far from over.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Spain wildfire kills several, with some victims found in vehicles
item.description: At least 11 people died in a wildfire near Almeria in southern Spain late on Thursday, regional authorities said, with some victims found inside vehicles. The country has suffered increasingly frequent and prolonged heatwaves, creating the conditions for major wildfires.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Dembélé happy to play in third consecutive World Cup semi final
item.description:
After scoring a goal for Les Bleus against Morocco on Thursday, French striker Ousmane Dembélé shifted focus to the next match, which would be his third consecutive FIFAWorldCup semi final.
The 2025 Ballon d'Or winner also had some words of praise for his captain and fellow goalscorer Kylian Mbappé ▶️
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Live: Iran buries Ali Khamenei after new fighting with US erupts
item.description: Iran on Friday laid to rest Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, more than four months after his death in an air strike, as two days of US and Iranian attacks raised fears of a return to all-out war. Khamenei's flag-covered coffin was carried into the shrine of Imam Reza in his home city of Mashhad in eastern Iran amid a sea of people, with no sign of a public appearance by his son and successor Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei. Follow our liveblog for all the latest updates.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Syria arrests IS group-linked suspects over Damascus bombings during Macron visit
item.description: Syrian authorities said on Thursday they had arrested several suspects accused of carrying out a series of bombings in Damascus including attacks during French President Emmanuel Macron's visit this week. The interior ministry said security forces dismantled the entire cell, while an official blamed the Islamic State group, which has not claimed responsibility.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé finds the answer as France advance past Morocco
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: World Cup 2026: Mbappé's breakthrough sends France past Morocco to semi-finals
item.description: France booked their place in the semi-finals for the third time in a row with a 2-0 victory on Thursday over Morocco, overcoming an early penalty miss to break down the resilient Atlas Lions. Kylian Mbappé's breakthrough goal and Ballon d'Or holder Ousmane Dembélé's finish proved decisive as the Bleus progressed to the World Cup's final four.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: UN 'deeply concerned' by ongoing drone attack in Sudan
item.description: In tonight's edition, the ICC is laying the groundwork for investigations into some of the atrocities committed during Sudan's brutal war. Also, rights activists, politicians, developers and business leaders are in Geneva for the AI for Good Summit. And, South African artist Credo V. Daniels has had his music removed from Apple Music after a performance divided fans, with some accusing him of relying too heavily on AI for his vocals.
item.dc:creator: Georja CALVIN-SMITH

item.title: World Cup: ATFrenchies share their France-Morocco predictions
item.description:
⚽ 🏆 Alexandre Durand and Tom Carles, the duo that make up AT Frenchies, are showing their support for France's #LesBleus as they face #Morocco in the first quarter final at the FIFA World Cup.
🇫🇷 🇲🇦 Who do they think will win? They share their analysis with FRANCE 24 ▶️
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Technical details need to be agreed before Ukraine can produce Patriot missiles, Zelensky says
item.description: Ukraine's President Volodymyr Zelensky on Thursday said that technical details still needed to be finalised with the US before Kyiv could start manufacturing its own Patriot air-defence missiles. Ukraine's dwindling interceptor stockpiles have been outstripped by Russia's deadly ballistic missile attacks.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Nike and Adidas locked in World Cup standoff as tournament heats up
item.description: As the World Cup quarterfinals get under way, the race to number one between Nike and Adidas heats up. India and Australia sign a new uranium deal. And, OpenAI rolls out its newest model GPT-5.6 Sol to the general public.
item.dc:creator: Camille KNIGHT

item.title: FIFA World Cup: Fake racism videos go viral as players, fans face real abuse
item.description: AI-generated fake videos, viral hoaxes and real racism are colliding at the FIFA World Cup. From a fabricated Ronald Koeman rant and false claims targeting Algerian fans to the racist abuse of Kylian Mbappé and FIFA investigations, misinformation is spreading alongside genuine discrimination. Vedika Bahl fact-checks the biggest World Cup racism claims, debunks the false ones, and separates AI-generated deception from real incidents of racist abuse on and off the pitch.
item.dc:creator: Vedika BAHL

item.title: Gazans mourn aid worker killed by Israel who organised public World Cup screenings
item.description: Crowds of Gazans took to the streets on Wednesday for the funeral of Palestinian aid worker Mohammad al-Waheidi, who was killed by an Israeli air strike Tuesday. Waheidi had been involved in setting up public screenings of World Cup matches in the devastated Palestinian territory.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
